description: VIZA AI visa application platform - Indonesian tourist visa B211A demo

userStories.title: Create VIZA database schema and Drizzle migrations
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a clean VIZA database schema so the app has the correct tables for visa applications.
acceptanceCriteria: Drizzle schema files created for: applicant_profiles, applications, application_documents, submission_queue, visa_chat_sessions, visa_chat_messages, visa_documents, visa_chunks
visa_chunks has embedding column of type vector(1536) for OpenAI text-embedding-3-small
applicant_profiles.language_pref defaults to 'en'
applications.country defaults to 'indonesia', visa_type defaults to 'tourist_b211a', status defaults to 'draft'
Drizzle config points to Supabase project: https://oyjxdzsoejraedqghndi.supabase.co
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Create schema files under src/db/ matching the VIZA data model. The Supabase project URL is https://oyjxdzsoejraedqghndi.supabase.co. The pgvector extension must be enabled on the Supabase project (add a note in README or migration). Use Drizzle ORM patterns. Do not touch any frontend files.

userStories.title: Write agent soul and wire Anthropic Claude SDK as AI provider
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the chatbot to use Claude as its AI model with a VIZA visa assistant persona.
acceptanceCriteria: src/agent/soul.md written for VIZA visa assistant - English only, warm and practical, guides applicants through Indonesian tourist visa application
Anthropic SDK (@anthropic-ai/sdk) is used directly for all AI calls
Model used is claude-sonnet-4-5
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use direct Anthropic SDK calls with claude-sonnet-4-5. The soul.md should describe: VIZA visa assistant, English Phase 1, warm tone, explains why each document is needed, does not make applicants feel stupid, knows Indonesian tourist visa B211A requirements.

userStories.title: Build knowledge domain with VisaKnowledgeService and OpenAI embeddings
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a RAG knowledge service that queries VIZA visa requirements using OpenAI text-embedding-3-small for semantic search.
acceptanceCriteria: VisaKnowledgeService created with OpenAI text-embedding-3-small (1536 dimensions) via openai npm package
Semantic search queries visa_chunks table via pgvector cosine similarity
Tool: query-visa-requirements({ country, visa_type, query }) returns relevant passages
Tool: get-document-checklist({ country, visa_type }) returns structured required documents list for Indonesia tourist_b211a
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The pgvector search should filter by country and visa_type metadata columns. Indonesia B211A required docs hardcoded as fallback if DB is empty: passport bio page, photo, flight booking, hotel booking, travel itinerary, bank statement.

userStories.title: Build domains/application - visa application state management tools
userStories.description: As a developer, I want agent tools to read and write visa application state from Supabase so the chatbot can track document collection progress.
acceptanceCriteria: domains/application created with visa application management tools
Tool: get-application-status({ applicationId }) returns current application row + all application_documents rows
Tool: update-application-field({ applicationId, field, value }) patches applications or applicant_profiles table
Tool: check-document-completeness({ applicationId }) returns list of missing required document types for Indonesia tourist_b211a
Tool: mark-document-received({ applicationId, documentType, storagePath }) inserts or updates application_documents row
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Required document types for Indonesia B211A: passport_copy, photo, flight_booking, hotel_booking, travel_itinerary, bank_statement. The check-document-completeness tool should compare uploaded docs against this list and return what is missing.

userStories.title: Build domains/submission, escalation, and conversation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the submission, escalation and conversation domains implemented for the VIZA visa context.
acceptanceCriteria: domains/submission created - tool: trigger-submission({ applicationId }) validates all docs present then inserts into submission_queue table with status='pending'
trigger-submission returns error if required documents are missing
domains/escalation created - escalate-to-agent tool for visa-related escalations
domains/conversation created with visa_chat_sessions and visa_chat_messages tables
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The trigger-submission tool must first call check-document-completeness - if anything is missing, return an error message listing what is needed. Only insert into submission_queue when all 6 required documents are present.

userStories.title: Implement Socket.io component protocol - shared TypeScript types
userStories.description: As a developer, I want typed definitions for the inline component events so both the backend and frontend share the same contract.
acceptanceCriteria: Shared types file defines ComponentEvent and ComponentCompleteEvent interfaces
ComponentEvent has: type='component', component (enum of all step + primitive component names), componentId (uuid), props (typed per component)
ComponentCompleteEvent has: type='component_complete', componentId, result

Component names enum includes: PersonalInfoStep, PassportStep, TravelInfoStep, DocumentUploadStep, ReviewStep, StatusStep, FileUploadCard, DatePickerCard, FormCard, DocumentChecklistCard, ConfirmationCard, StatusCard
Backend emits ComponentEvent via Socket.io when agent tool requests a component render
Backend receives ComponentCompleteEvent and passes result back to agent as tool response
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Add a types/components.ts file with the full component protocol types. Update the websocket handler to emit component events from the agent and receive component_complete events from the client. The agent should be able to call a tool like render-component({ component: 'PassportStep', props: { prefill: {...} } }) which triggers the Socket.io emit.

userStories.title: Frontend foundation - apply VIZA brand: fonts, colors, border radius, layout
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the frontend to use VIZA brand identity with Switzer/Inter fonts and navy color palette.
acceptanceCriteria: app/fonts.ts uses next/font/local for Switzer (public/fonts/switzer/Switzer-Variable.woff2) and Inter (public/fonts/inter/Inter-Variable.ttf)
app/layout.tsx applies font CSS variables: className includes switzer.variable and inter.variable on body
globals.css h1-h6 use var(--font-switzer), body uses var(--font-inter)
tailwind.config.ts brand color #03346E as brand-500/DEFAULT, full scale from 50-900
tailwind.config.ts --radius is 0.25rem (lg=4px, md=3px, sm=2px)
tailwind.config.ts font-heading/font-switzer = Switzer, font-sans/font-inter = Inter
App name set to 'VIZA' in metadata, title tags, layout
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Font files are already in place: public/fonts/switzer/Switzer-Variable.woff2 and public/fonts/inter/Inter-Variable.ttf. The tailwind brand scale: 50=#EEF3FA, 100=#D4E0F0, 200=#AABFDF, 300=#7A9DCE, 400=#3D6DAD, 500=#03346E, 600=#022B5C, 700=#01214A, 800=#011737, 900=#000D21. Preserve existing layout structure, spacing, component hierarchy, and sizing. Only change: font families, brand color, and app name text.

userStories.title: Build login page and /onboarding multi-step form
userStories.description: As a new user, I want to log in with VIZA branding and complete an onboarding form so my applicant profile is saved before I start the visa application.
acceptanceCriteria: Login page styled with VIZA branding (logo text, page title, button labels)
/client/onboarding page built with 4 steps: (1) full name/DOB/nationality, (2) passport number/issue date/expiry/issuing country, (3) travel dates/purpose, (4) email/phone/WeChat
On completion, data saved to applicant_profiles table via Supabase
onboarding_done set to true in applicant_profiles on completion
Returning users who have completed onboarding are redirected past /onboarding
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Preserve existing layout structure, spacing, card sizes, form field sizing, and visual hierarchy. Use VIZA brand colors and fonts. The onboarding page should use a step-by-step multi-step structure with visa applicant fields. Do not build the backend API - use Supabase client directly from the frontend.

userStories.title: Build shared application step components
userStories.description: As a developer, I want self-contained step components that work both in the /application page and as inline chat components.
acceptanceCriteria: components/application-steps/ directory created with 6 components: PersonalInfoStep, PassportStep, TravelInfoStep, DocumentUploadStep, ReviewStep, StatusStep
Each component accepts: data props (prefill values), applicationId, onComplete callback (called with collected data)
PersonalInfoStep fields: fullName, dateOfBirth, placeOfBirth, gender, nationality, occupation, address
PassportStep fields: passportNumber, issueDate, expiryDate, issuingCountry, issuingAuthority
TravelInfoStep fields: arrivalDate, departureDate, portOfEntry, purpose, accommodationName, accommodationAddress
DocumentUploadStep renders one FileUploadCard per required document type (passport_copy, photo, flight_booking, hotel_booking, travel_itinerary, bank_statement)
ReviewStep renders read-only summary of all filled data with Edit buttons per section
StatusStep renders submission confirmation: confirmationNumber, submittedAt, estimatedProcessingDays, receiptUrl
All components use Shadcn UI, Switzer font for headings, Inter for body
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow the established visual patterns in the codebase: same card padding, same label/input sizing, same button styles, same spacing scale. Use Shadcn UI components. Switzer for headings, Inter for body/labels. Brand color #03346E for primary actions. DocumentUploadStep should upload to Supabase Storage bucket 'application-documents' under path: {userId}/{applicationId}/{documentType}/{filename}.

userStories.title: Build /application page - step-by-step visa portal flow
userStories.description: As an applicant, I want a structured step-by-step page that mirrors the evisa.imigrasi.go.id portal flow so I can fill my application directly.
acceptanceCriteria: app/client/application/page.tsx created
Step progress bar at top showing all 6 steps with status: Complete / In Progress / Locked
Steps mounted in sequence using the 6 components from US-009
Current step state persisted to Supabase (applications + applicant_profiles + application_documents tables)
Completed steps show a summary with Edit button to return
Locked steps show a disabled preview
Navigation sidebar updated to include /application link
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Build following the existing visual patterns of the codebase - same card styles, same spacing, same button sizing as seen in other client pages. The step progress bar should use the same visual language as existing progress indicators in the app. Use brand-500 (#03346E) for completed step indicators.

userStories.title: Build /home application dashboard
userStories.description: As an applicant, I want a dashboard home page showing my active visa application status and quick actions.
acceptanceCriteria: app/client/home/page.tsx built as VIZA application dashboard
Active application card: Indonesia flag, visa type label, status badge (Draft/In Progress/Submitted/Approved/Rejected) with appropriate colors
Progress bar: count of uploaded documents vs 6 required, percentage complete
Quick action buttons: 'Continue with AI' links to /client/chat, 'Upload Documents' links to /client/application step 4, 'View Application' links to /client/application
Recent activity feed: last 5 events from application_documents uploads and application status changes
Empty state for users with no application: CTA button 'Start your Indonesia visa application'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Preserve the overall layout structure - card grid layout, sidebar + main content split, spacing, and visual hierarchy. Status badge colors: Draft=gray, In Progress=brand-400, Submitted=brand-500, Approved=green, Rejected=red. Keep any skeleton/loading states that already exist.

userStories.title: Build /chat VIZA AI chatbot with inline component rendering
userStories.description: As an applicant, I want to chat with the VIZA AI assistant and have it render interactive forms and upload cards inline in the conversation.
acceptanceCriteria: app/client/chat/page.tsx created with full chat interface
Socket.io connection to chatbot backend with streaming token support
Incoming component events from backend render the matching React component inline in the chat thread
component_complete events sent back to backend when user submits a step component
Supports all 6 step components (PersonalInfoStep etc) and 6 primitive components (FileUploadCard etc) as inline renders
Session history sidebar shows past conversations
English only - no language switching UI
VIZA assistant name and avatar used
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Preserve the chat UI structure - message layout, input area sizing, sidebar width, message bubble styles, streaming animation. When a Socket.io 'component' event arrives, render the matching React component inline in the message thread between messages. When onComplete fires, emit component_complete back to server. Import step components from components/application-steps/.

userStories.title: Build /documents page and /settings page
userStories.description: As an applicant, I want to see all my uploaded documents with their status, and manage my account settings.
acceptanceCriteria: app/client/documents/page.tsx created with document management interface
Document grid: one card per document type (6 required), shows filename/thumbnail if uploaded, missing state if not
Each card shows status badge: uploaded / validated / missing / rejected
Rejected cards show rejection reason
Upload button on missing/rejected cards opens file picker and uploads to Supabase Storage
Progress summary at top: X of 6 documents uploaded
app/client/settings page styled with VIZA branding and page title 'Account Settings'
Settings page: profile info edit, password change, notification preferences - no language toggle (Phase 2)
Navigation sidebar updated with correct links: Home, Application, Chat, Documents, Settings
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: For /documents, use a grid/card layout structure with document type cards. Keep consistent card dimensions and grid columns. For /settings, minimal changes - preserve the settings layout, update text labels. For navigation sidebar: preserve the sidebar structure and spacing, set nav items to the 5 VIZA pages (Home, Application, Chat, Documents, Settings). Do not change sidebar width, padding, or icon sizing.

userStories.title: Build knowledge base ingest script for Indonesia B211A
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a script that scrapes Indonesian tourist visa requirements and ingests them into the visa_chunks table with OpenAI embeddings.
acceptanceCriteria: knowledge-base/scripts/ingest-indonesia-tourist.ts created
Script fetches content from evisa.imigrasi.go.id and imigrasi.go.id
Content chunked into paragraphs (~500 tokens each)
Each chunk embedded using OpenAI text-embedding-3-small (1536 dim)
Chunks inserted into visa_documents + visa_chunks tables with country='indonesia', visa_type='tourist_b211a'
document_type assigned per chunk: requirements | process | faq | form_fields | common_mistakes
Script runnable via: cd knowledge-base && npx ts-node scripts/ingest-indonesia-tourist.ts
Existing chunks for same country/visa_type deleted before re-ingesting (idempotent)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Create the knowledge-base/ package from scratch if it does not exist. Use the openai npm package for embeddings. The SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, and OPENAI_API_KEY are available as env vars. Include a hardcoded fallback dataset of Indonesia B211A requirements in case scraping fails (the 6 required documents + fees + processing time).

userStories.title: Build browser automation submission service
userStories.description: As a visa agency, I want an automated service that fills and submits the Indonesian e-visa application on behalf of the applicant.
acceptanceCriteria: viza-be/submission-service/ created as a standalone Node.js + TypeScript service
package.json with playwright, @supabase/supabase-js, dotenv dependencies
Main loop polls submission_queue table for status='pending' items every 30 seconds
For each pending item: loads applicant_profiles + applications + application_documents from Supabase
Downloads documents from Supabase Storage to temp directory
Playwright opens evisa.imigrasi.go.id and attempts to fill the application form
On success: updates applications.status='submitted', applications.confirmation_number, applications.submitted_at
On success: marks submission_queue item as done
On failure: increments attempts, sets last_error, retries up to 3 times
After 3 failures: sends alert email via Resend API (RESEND_API_KEY in env)
README documents how to run and deploy to Railway
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Create this as a new standalone service directory. It runs independently of the chatbot backend. The Playwright automation will need to map evisa.imigrasi.go.id form fields to applicant data. Since the portal may change, build the form-fill logic as a separate mappings file that is easy to update. If the portal requires account creation or login, scaffold that step too but add a TODO comment. RESEND_API_KEY is available as an env var. The alert email should go to the service operator (hardcode edward.zehua.zhang@gmail.com for now).
